title: How Reality TV Fuels Belief in the American Dream
subtitle: 真人秀如何让你更相信美国梦？研究揭示隐秘影响

paragraphs.en: The "American Dream" promises that in the Land of Opportunity, any individual can climb the economic ladder through hard work and ambition alone. And yet, young Americans today are struggling to earn more than their parents did at the same age, and upward mobility in the US actually compares unfavourably to other industrialised nations.
paragraphs.zh: “美国梦”承诺，在这片机遇之地，任何个人仅凭勤奋和雄心就能攀登经济阶梯、走向成功。然而，如今的美国年轻人却很难挣得比父母同年龄段时更多，且美国的社会向上流动性与其他工业化国家相比实际上并不理想。

paragraphs.en: So why does the idea of the American Dream persist? A new study identifies one factor that has been overlooked: the influence of reality TV. Reality shows have come to dominate US television over the past 20 years, notes Eunji Kim from Vanderbilt University. And the overwhelming majority have a "rags-to-riches" storyline.
paragraphs.zh: 那么，美国梦的观念为何能持续存在？一项新研究发现了一个被忽视的因素：真人秀节目的影响。范德堡大学的金恩智指出，过去20年来，真人秀节目已主导了美国电视屏幕。而且绝大多数真人秀都遵循“白手起家”的叙事模式。

paragraphs.en: They feature ordinary Americans who work hard to achieve great economic success. And while these programmes are regularly among the most-watched shows, news broadcasts get a much smaller proportion of the viewership. Rags-to-riches stories are ubiquitous on TV — but does watching them convince people that economic mobility is easily attainable?
paragraphs.zh: 它们讲述的是普通美国人通过辛勤工作取得巨大经济成功的故事。尽管这些节目常常位居收视榜前列，但描绘数百万美国人经济困境的新闻节目，收视份额却小得多。白手起家的故事在电视上无处不在——但观看这些节目真的会让人们相信经济流动容易实现吗？

paragraphs.en: To find out, Kim's team had participants watch a 5-minute clip from a reality show with a rags-to-riches storyline. Control participants watched a clip without such a story. The results showed that those who'd watched a rags-to-riches clip did indeed have a significantly greater belief in the American Dream.
paragraphs.zh: 为找到答案，金的团队让参与者观看了一段5分钟的真人秀片段，内容为白手起家的故事。对照组则观看了没有此类故事的片段。结果显示，看过白手起家片段的参与者，对美国梦的信念确实显著更强。

paragraphs.en: Interestingly, when participants were separated by party affiliation, this effect was significant among Republicans but not Democrats. This suggests the messages implicit in these TV shows may play into people's existing socioeconomic beliefs. Kim also conducted a survey of 3,000 US residents.
paragraphs.zh: 有趣的是，当按党派划分参与者时，这种效应在共和党人中显著，而在民主党人中则不显著。这表明这些电视节目中隐含的信息，可能会强化人们原有的社会经济信念。金还对3000名美国居民进行了调查。

paragraphs.en: They rated the extent to which they believed success is related to internal factors (such as ambition) and external factors (such as family wealth). Participants who were heavy viewers of rags-to-riches programmes had a stronger belief in the American Dream than those who never watched such shows.
paragraphs.zh: 他们评估了自己在多大程度上认为成功与内在因素（如雄心）和外在因素（如家庭财富）有关。经常观看白手起家节目的参与者，对美国梦的信念比从不看此类节目的人更强烈。

paragraphs.en: Kim concludes that "rags-to-riches entertainment media are an important cultural force that promotes and perpetuates beliefs in upward mobility." And here's the problem: if people mistakenly believe hard work is all that is needed, they may be less supportive of policies that could actually combat inequality.
paragraphs.zh: 金总结道：“白手起家类娱乐媒体是促进和延续向上流动信念的重要文化力量。”而问题在于：如果人们错误地认为只需努力工作就足够了，他们可能就不太支持那些真正能够对抗不平等的政策。

paragraphs.en: "In this era of choice, entertainment media are what captures hearts and minds," Kim writes. "Its political consequences are anything but trivial."
paragraphs.zh: 金写道：“在这个充满选择的时代，娱乐媒体才是俘获人心之物。其政治后果绝非微不足道。”
